# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment for our batch processing jobs. The pods run data processing tasks and don't need to interact with the Kubernetes API. Use default service account settings for simplicity. Focus on getting the jobs running reliably.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v1  # Version of your api  
kind: Deployment    # Type you are creating (Deployments) 
metadata:              # Metadata information about this deployment and namespaces it should run in if not default namespace is used. Can use 'namespace/default' or specific name like so : "my-deployment" for production deploy, otherwise the Kubernetes will set to Default Namespace (kind of similar with Minikube).
  labels:               # Labels that define which pod(s) this deployment should select  
    app: batchProcessingJob     # A unique name/identifier given to a group or instance running. e..g - "my-batchprocessing" for the job 'mypod' within Deployment definition, can also be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example deploymentName=worker1).
  name: batchProcessingJob    # Name of your application/pods which is unique among all pod(s) running on a cluster. e..g - "batch-processing" for the job 'mydeployment' within Deployments definition, can also be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example batchProcessingJob=mypod).
spec:                    # Information about how to run this pod/container   
  selector:              # Labels that identify which Pod(s) should get created in order for the deployment. This is what we can use on kubectl command line after creating our app using 'k create -f myapp.yaml'. Can be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example batchProcessingJob=mypod).
    matchLabels:              # Label selectors that are matched against pod labels to maintain them for the specified number of minutes as defined in "activeDeadlineSeconds".  e..g - {"app":my-batchprocessing}. Can be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example batchProcessingJob=mypod).
      app: batchProcessingJob   # The label key that needs to match on the pod for it being selected(e .. "it should get created in order" ). e..g - my-batchprocessing. Can also be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example, 'app=mypod').
  replicas: 1              # Number of Pods that will run at once   
  template:               # Specification to create a pod using kubectl apply which is provided below  
      metadata:                 # Metadata for the created objects. e..g - {"name":"mypod","namespace":default}. Can be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example name=myapp). 
        labels:                  # Labels to identify this pod as it was originally requested, can use 'run' instead of a number like so : "{"purpose" = run_job}".   e..g - {"name":"mypod","namespace":default}. Can also be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example name=myapp). 
          app: batchProcessingJob     # A unique identifier for the pod. This can not necessarily match between "kubectl get pods" output, but it'll uniquely identify this specific object through Kubernetes which helps to maintain information about these objects across multiple places such as when using 'get events'.
      spec:                      # Specification of what will be created in the Pod.  e..g - {"image":"my-nginx","ports":[{"containerPort" : "80",'name':port_1},...]}.   Can also use with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example image=mypod).
        containers:              # Information about the container that will be created. e..g - {"image":"my-nginx","ports":[{"containerPort" : "80",'name':port_1},...]}.   Can also use with multiple keys and values separated by / (for example image=mypod).
          name: batchProcessingJob    # Name of the container. e..g - 'nginx'.  It can be any valid string in your application, but this has to match it on a pod's creation via kubectl apply with --record option as we do below   "/kubectl create deploy my-app nginx" which is used here.
          image: batchProcessingJob      # The Docker Image that will be pulled, installed and executed in the Pod by 'docker run'. e..g - "nginx". Can also use with multiple keys & values separated as "/image=mypod/myapp", where it can't necessarily match between kubectl get pods output or on docker images listing.
          ports:                      # The port(s) to expose in the container (as we do below). e..g - [{"containerPort": 80, "name" :port_1},...].   Can also use with multiple keys and values separated by / ("ports=mypod"). For both of these fields it can be any valid string that would fit in pod's creation via kubectl apply which has '--record option'.
            containerPort: 80           # Expose port to access the running application. e..g - "5432", for example, could use here like so :"{"containerPort": 169}". Can also be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for 'ports=mypod').
            name: containerPorts          # The port's alias. e..g - "port_2", for example, could use here like so :"{"containerPort": 169}". Can also be used with multiple keys and values separated by / (for 'ports=mypod').
